element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Environmental puzzles tied to family member rescue

element_introduction_order.element: Boss encounters with family members held hostage

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Health refill currency or game progression
Activated by Lurch's music room tune after other rescues

typical_relief_triggers: Secret areas with power-ups and lives
typical_tension_triggers: Multiple enemy types in single screen
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Non-linear exploration-based with optional area discovery; tension builds through environmental hazards and enemy encounters; relief through resource collection and story progression via family member rescues

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Invisible doors leading to secret areas with concentrated resources (Pugsley's Den contains power-ups, money, 5 1-UPs, and hidden 27-life area)
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Invisible door mechanic; unnoticeable bonus spots in non-secret locations; Thing boxes hint at hidden mechanics and navigation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Basic enemy and platforming introduction in accessible mansion areas; health system starts with 2 heart containers
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Hazard complexity increases (fire lakes, pendulums); puzzle-solving integrated with family member rescues (ice area, stove area); boss encounters scattered throughout
difficulty_curve.late_game: Underground chambers require coordination (all other family members must be rescued first and meet in Music Room); The Judge as final boss; Morticia rescue as story climax
notable_spikes: First boss encounter introducing required defeat mechanic
Ice-themed area combining hazards with puzzle-solving
Underground chamber activation requiring prerequisite completions

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Hub-based exploration from Hall of Stairs
notable_level_patterns.description: Six doorways lead to distinct themed areas (kitchen, games room, portrait gallery, etc.); each room features approximately 40-50 screens; non-linear order allows player choice
Distinct themed areas with integrated environmental puzzles tied to specific rescues; examples include ice area for Wednesday and stove area for Granny
Invisible doors and hidden spots concentrate power-ups, money, and multiple extra lives in single locations
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Pugsley's Den (invisible door with 5 1-UPs and 27-life secret area)

notable_level_patterns.description: Bosses hold family members hostage; defeating them is narrative and mechanical requirement; includes environmental boss (giant bird in garden)
Underground chambers only activate after all other family members rescued and Lurch plays music room tune with full party present
